title: eriocycla thomsonii
intro: Eriocycla thomsonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the 'Peach-flower', native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, derived from its essential oils and extracts. The health benefits of Eriocycla thomsonii include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Eriocycla thomsonii is often prepared as a herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture, and can also be used in traditional medicine as a paste or powder, showcasing its versatility in various herbal preparations.
